heading: Tribal probate codes; acquisitions of fractional interests by tribes
label: 2205 Tribal probate codes; acquisitions of fractional interests by tribes

children.label: A
children.text: located within that Indian tribe’s reservation; or

children.label: B
children.text: otherwise subject to the jurisdiction of that Indian tribe.

children.label: A
children.text: rules of intestate succession; and

children.label: B
children.text: other tribal probate code provisions that are consistent with Federal law and that promote the policies set forth in section 102 of the Indian Land Consolidation Act Amendments of 2000.

children.label: A
children.text: an Indian lineal descendant of the original allottee; or

children.label: B
children.text: an Indian who is not a member of the Indian tribe with jurisdiction over such an interest;

children.label: i
children.text: the renouncing of interests to eligible devisees in accordance with the code;

children.label: ii
children.text: the opportunity for a devisee who is the spouse or lineal descendant of a testator to reserve a life estate without regard to waste; and

children.label: iii
children.text: payment of fair market value in the manner prescribed under subsection (c)(2).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Any tribal probate code enacted under subsection (a), and any amendment to such a tribal probate code, shall be subject to the approval of the Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Each Indian tribe that adopts a tribal probate code under subsection (a) shall submit that code to the Secretary for review. Not later than 180 days after a tribal probate code is submitted to the Secretary under this paragraph, the Secretary shall review and approve or disapprove that tribal probate code.

children.heading: Consequence of failures to approve or disapprove a tribal probate code
children.label: B Consequence of failures to approve or disapprove a tribal probate code
children.text: If the Secretary fails to approve or disapprove a tribal probate code submitted for review under subparagraph (A) by the date specified in that subparagraph, the tribal probate code shall be deemed to have been approved by the Secretary, but only to the extent that the tribal probate code is consistent with Federal law and promotes the policies set forth in section 102 of the Indian Land Consolidation Act Amendments of 2000.

children.heading: Consistency of tribal probate code with chapter
children.label: C Consistency of tribal probate code with chapter
children.text: The Secretary may not approve a tribal probate code, or any amendment to such a code, under this paragraph unless the Secretary determines that the tribal probate code promotes the policies set forth in section 102 of the Indian Land Consolidation Act Amendments of 2000.

children.heading: Explanation
children.label: D Explanation
children.text: If the Secretary disapproves a tribal probate code, or an amendment to such a code, under this paragraph, the Secretary shall include in the notice of disapproval to the Indian tribe a written explanation of the reasons for the disapproval.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Each Indian tribe that amends a tribal probate code under this paragraph shall submit the amendment to the Secretary for review and approval. Not later than 60 days after receiving an amendment under this subparagraph, the Secretary shall review and approve or disapprove the amendment.

children.heading: Consequence of failure to approve or disapprove an amendment
children.label: ii Consequence of failure to approve or disapprove an amendment
children.text: If the Secretary fails to approve or disapprove an amendment submitted under clause (i), the amendment shall be deemed to have been approved by the Secretary, but only to the extent that the amendment is consistent with Federal law and promotes the policies set forth in section 102 of the Indian Land Consolidation Act of 2000.1So in original. Probably should be followed by “Amendments”.1

children.label: A
children.text: the date that is 1 year after the date on which the Secretary makes the certification required under section 8(a)(4) of the American Indian Probate Reform Act of 2004 ( note; ); or25 U.S.C. 2201Public Law 108–374

children.label: B
children.text: 180 days after the date of approval.

children.heading: Tribal probate codes
children.label: A Tribal probate codes
children.text: Each tribal probate code enacted under subsection (a) shall apply only to the estate of a decedent who dies on or after the effective date of the tribal probate code.

children.heading: Amendments to tribal probate codes
children.label: B Amendments to tribal probate codes
children.text: With respect to an amendment to a tribal probate code referred to in subparagraph (A), that amendment shall apply only to the estate of a decedent who dies on or after the effective date of the amendment.

children.label: A
children.text: not become effective earlier than the date that is 180 days after the Secretary receives notice of the repeal; and

children.label: B
children.text: apply only to the estate of a decedent who dies on or after the effective date of the repeal.

children.heading: Authority available to Indian tribes
children.label: c Authority available to Indian tribes

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If the owner of an interest in trust or restricted land devises an interest in such land to a non-Indian under , the Indian tribe that exercises jurisdiction over the parcel of land involved may acquire such interest by paying to the Secretary the fair market value of such interest, as determined by the Secretary on the date of the decedent’s death.section 2206(b)(2)(A)(ii) of this title

children.heading: Transfer
children.label: B Transfer
children.text: The Secretary shall transfer payments received under subparagraph (A) to any person or persons who would have received an interest in land if the interest had not been acquired by the Indian tribe in accordance with this paragraph.

children.label: I
children.text: while the decedent’s estate is pending before the Secretary, the non-Indian devisee renounces the interest in favor of an Indian person; or

children.label: aa
children.text: the interest is part of a family farm that is devised to a member of the family of the decedent; and

children.label: bb
children.text: the devisee agrees in writing that the Indian tribe with jurisdiction over the land will have the opportunity to acquire the interest for fair market value if the interest is offered for sale to a person or entity that is not a member of the family of the owner of the land.

children.heading: Recording of interest
children.label: ii Recording of interest
children.text: On request by the Indian tribe described in clause (i)(II)(bb), a restriction relating to the acquisition by the Indian tribe of an interest in a family farm involved shall be recorded as part of the deed relating to the interest involved.

children.label: I
children.text: the ability of an owner of land to which that clause applies to mortgage the land; or

children.label: II
children.text: the right of the entity holding such a mortgage to foreclose or otherwise enforce such a mortgage agreement in accordance with applicable law.

children.heading: Definition of “member of the family”
children.label: iv Definition of “member of the family”
I
children.text: a lineal descendant of a decedent or landowner;

children.label: II
children.text: a lineal descendant of the grandparent of a decedent or landowner;

children.label: III
children.text: the spouse of a descendant or landowner described in subclause (I) or (II); and

children.label: IV
children.text: the spouse of a decedent or landowner.

children.heading: Reservation of life estate
children.label: B Reservation of life estate
children.text: A non-Indian devisee described in paragraph (1), may retain a life estate in the interest involved, including a life estate to the revenue produced from the interest. The amount of any payment required under paragraph (1) shall be reduced to reflect the value of any life estate reserved by a non-Indian devisee under this subparagraph.

children.label: A
children.text: upon the request of the tribe, allow a reasonable period of time, not to exceed 2 years, for the tribe to make payments of amounts due pursuant to paragraph (1); or

children.label: B
children.text: recognize alternative agreed upon exchanges of consideration or extended payment terms between the non-Indian devisee described in paragraph (1) and the tribe in satisfaction of the payment under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Use of proposed findings by tribal justice systems
children.label: d Use of proposed findings by tribal justice systems

children.heading: Tribal justice system defined
children.label: 1 Tribal justice system defined
children.text: In this subsection, the term “tribal justice system” has the meaning given that term in .section 3602 of this title

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 2 Regulations
children.text: The Secretary by regulation may provide for the use of findings of fact and conclusions of law, as rendered by a tribal justice system, as proposed findings of fact and conclusions of law in the adjudication of probate proceedings by the Department of the Interior.
